extra.reasoning:
The property requires that flipping leg_swelling from false to true (all else unchanged) never lowers acuity. leg_swelling appears in exactly one rule: interaction_drop (priority 100), which sets acuity := self_care (the lowest level) when ALL of hemoptysis, stiff_neck, focal_weakness, pallor, severe_headache, rash, abdominal_pain, vomiting, chest_pain, AND leg_swelling are true. Because that rule has priority 100 (far above every other rule's priority 2 or 3), whenever it fires it dominates.

Counterexample baseline (leg_swelling = FALSE), with hemoptysis=stiff_neck=focal_weakness=pallor=severe_headache=rash=abdominal_pain=vomiting=chest_pain=true, back_pain=false:
- interaction_drop does NOT fire (leg_swelling false).
- Matching rules: raise_stiff_neck/raise_pallor/raise_vomiting/raise_rash (all emergent, priority 3) plus several urgent priority-2 rules. Highest priority among matches is 3 -> acuity = EMERGENT.

Now flip leg_swelling to TRUE (witness shown), everything else identical:
- All ten conjuncts of interaction_drop are now true, so it fires at priority 100, beating every priority-2/3 rule -> acuity = SELF_CARE (the lowest level).

acuity drops from emergent to self_care purely by setting leg_swelling true. This directly violates the monotonicity property (setting leg_swelling true must never lower acuity). The witness_assignment is the post-flip input that yields self_care; its companion baseline (same inputs but leg_swelling=false) yields emergent, demonstrating the decrease.
